query: Voyager 1 February 25 2025 cosmic ray subsystem shutdown power conservation NASA update

{"query":"Voyager 1 February 25 2025 cosmic ray subsystem shutdown power conservation NASA update","model":"mako","response_time_ms":2381,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.jpl.nasa.gov/news/nasa-turns-off-two-voyager-science-instruments-to-extend-mission/","title":"NASA Turns Off 2 Voyager Science Instruments to Extend Mission | NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL)","content":"The cosmic ray subsystem that was shut down on Voyager 1 last week is a suite of three telescopes designed to study cosmic rays, including protons from the galaxy and the Sun, by measuring their energy and flux.
“Every bit of additional data we have gathered since then is not only valuable bonus science for heliophysics, but also a testament to the exemplary engineering that has gone into the Voyagers — starting nearly 50 years ago and continuing to this day.”
Its cosmic ray subsystem is scheduled to be shut off in 2026.
With the implementation of this power conservation plan, engineers believe the two probes could have enough electricity to continue operating with at least one science instrument into the 2030s.
But they are also mindful that the Voyagers have been weathering deep space for 47 years and that unforeseen challenges could shorten that timeline."},{"url":"https://science.nasa.gov/blogs/voyager/2026/04/17/nasa-shuts-off-instrument-on-voyager-1-to-keep-spacecraft-operating/","title":"NASA Shuts Off Instrument on Voyager 1 to Keep Spacecraft Operating - NASA Science","content":"The twin Voyagers are the only spacecraft that are far enough from Earth to provide this information.
Because Voyager 1 is more than 15 billion miles (25 billion kilometers) from Earth, the sequence of commands to shut down the instrument will take 23 or so hours to reach the spacecraft, and the shutdown process itself will take about three hours and 15 minutes to complete.
They are using the time to finalize a more ambitious energy-saving fix for both Voyagers they call “the Big Bang,” which is designed to further extend Voyager operations.
Tests are planned for May and June 2026."},{"url":"https://www.sciencealert.com/nasa-shuts-down-another-piece-of-voyager-1-to-extend-its-interstellar-mission","title":"NASA Shuts Down Another Piece of Voyager 1 to Extend Its Interstellar Mission : ScienceAlert","content":"The reason for this shutdown is that Voyager's mission team wants to conserve power, which the aging spacecraft is in short supply of.
For Voyager 1, the LECP was next on the list, which had already been shut off on Voyager 2 back in March 2025.
In the meantime, the engineers are taking the time this provides to finalize a larger strategy to extend the mission, which they call the " Big Bang.""},{"url":"https://www.cnn.com/2025/03/05/science/voyager-probes-turn-off-instruments","title":"Voyager probes shut off instruments to conserve power | CNN","content":"NASA’s twin Voyager probes, which launched 47 years ago, are shutting off some science instruments in an effort to conserve power and keep their storied missions going.
Engineers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Pasadena, California, sent a command for Voyager 1 to power down its cosmic ray subsystem experiment on February 25.
Voyager 1 is more than 15 billion miles (25 billion kilometers) from Earth, while Voyager 2 is over 13 billion miles (21 billion kilometers) away, according to NASA."},{"url":"https://www.spacewar.com/reports/NASA_Turns_Off_Voyager_Science_Instruments_to_Prolong_Mission_999.html","title":"NASA Turns Off Voyager Science Instruments to Prolong Mission","content":"NASA has implemented energy-saving measures to keep the Voyager spacecraft operational for as long as possible, despite their decreasing power supply.
Last October, the plasma science instrument aboard Voyager 2 was turned off to conserve power.
"The Voyager spacecraft far exceeded their original planetary mission," said Patrick Koehn, Voyager program scientist at NASA Headquarters in Washington.
Voyager 2 will continue running its magnetic field and plasma wave instruments, with its cosmic ray subsystem planned for shutdown in 2026."}]}

The twin Voyager probes have been exploring space for 47 years. NASA’s twin Voyager probes, which launched 47 years ago, are shutting off some science instruments in an effort to conserve power and keep their storied missions going. Voyager 1 and Voyager 2 are both exploring uncharted territory in interstellar space. Engineers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Pasadena, California, sent a command for Voyager 1 to power down its cosmic ray subsystem experiment on February 25. Meanwhile, Voyager 2’s low-energy charged particle instrument will shut off on March 24. And Voyager 1’s identical instrument has been shut off for years because its performance degraded over time. After turning off the two instruments, both Voyager probes should be able to operate for another year before the team will need to shut off an additional instrument for each spacecraft.","score":0.89279664,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.sciencealert.com/nasa-shuts-down-another-piece-of-voyager-1-to-extend-its-interstellar-mission","title":"NASA Shuts Down Another Piece of Voyager 1 to Extend Its Interstellar Mission : ScienceAlert","content":"# NASA Shuts Down Another Piece of Voyager 1 to Extend Its Interstellar Mission. NASA Shuts Down Another Piece of Voyager 1 to Extend Its Interstellar Mission  (NASA/JPL-Caltech). The farthest spacecraft from Earth, the Voyager 1 probe, has just shut down another instrument. The reason for this shutdown is that Voyager's mission team wants to conserve power, which the aging spacecraft is in short supply of. Basically, the decision was made to power down this instrument so humanity's first interstellar mission could continue exploring the Universe. Engineers at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) sent commands to shut down the instrument on April 17th, following the shutdown of the Cosmic Ray Subsystem (CRS) in February 2025 and the imaging system, ultraviolet spectrometer, and other instruments since 1990. This is a careful balance that requires the mission team to determine which instruments can be sacrificed at this point in the mission. NASA Shuts Down Another Voyager 1 Instrument to Keep The Mission Going.","score":0.87807417,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.jpl.nasa.gov/news/nasa-turns-off-two-voyager-science-instruments-to-extend-mission","title":"NASA Turns Off 2 Voyager Science Instruments to Extend Mission | NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL)","content":"# NASA Turns Off 2 Voyager Science Instruments to Extend Mission. Mission engineers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California turned off the cosmic ray subsystem experiment aboard Voyager 1 on Feb. 25 and will shut off Voyager 2’s low-energy charged particle instrument on March 24. Three science instruments will continue to operate on each spacecraft. Launched in 1977, Voyagers 1 and 2 rely on a radioisotope power system that generates electricity from the heat of decaying plutonium. Last October, to conserve energy, the project turned off Voyager 2’s plasma science instrument, which measures the amount of plasma — electrically charged atoms — and the direction it is flowing.
